title: OpenShift Cluster Monitoring Operator and In-Cluster Observability Plane: Prometheus, Alertmanager, Thanos, Telemeter, Insights, and User Workload Monitoring Health with Self-Monitoring Drift
exclusions: Does not replace UC-3.3.6 generic ClusterOperator health across all platform operators without monitoring operand depth, UC-3.3.10 storage and CSI analytics, UC-3.3.12 OVN-Kubernetes cluster network health, UC-3.3.19 IngressController operator reconciliation, UC-3.3.14 OAuth authentication flows, UC-3.3.20 cluster certificate expiry inventories, or vanilla application-level Prometheus instances outside the platform monitoring stack. Does not automatically restart prometheus or alertmanager pods; it produces evidence and routing guidance for human-approved platform operations.
evidence: Saved search openshift_uc_3_3_2_cmo_observability_plane_health with fifteen minute schedule; Splunk OpenShift App drilldowns on monitoring ClusterOperator snapshots, prometheus lines, lookup joins, events, and audit rows; alert stanza with observability routing keys; weekly CSV exports to a restricted evidence index.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab OpenShift cluster, ingest ocp_clusteroperator rows where monitoring reports Degraded=True with message text referencing prometheus-k8s or alertmanager-main, add matching prometheus lines with kube_statefulset_status_replicas_ready below expected or prometheus_rule_evaluation_failures_total above zero, execute openshift_uc_3_3_2_cmo_observability_plane_health, and confirm page or warn severity with non-null recommended_action within the scheduled window.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After recovery when oc get clusteroperator monitoring shows Available=True without Degraded, openshift-monitoring StatefulSets show full readiness, remote_write failure counters return to baseline, rule evaluation failures clear, and Watchdog samples appear, confirm severities return to info and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to page and warn only.

dataSources: index=ocp_metrics sourcetype=ocp_clusteroperator for oc get clusteroperator monitoring snapshots with Available Progressing Degraded Upgradeable conditions and messages; index=ocp_metrics sourcetype=prometheus:metrics or sourcetype=prometheus:ocp for kube_pod_status_ready kube_statefulset_status_replicas_ready up prometheus_rule_evaluation_failures_total ALERTS for Watchdog remote_storage and metricsclient failure counters alertmanager notification counters optional thanos store error series filtered to openshift-monitoring and openshift-user-workload-monitoring; index=ocp_events sourcetype=ocp_events for monitoring namespace rollouts and probe failures; index=ocp_audit sourcetype=ocp_audit for cluster-monitoring-config and user-workload-monitoring-config mutations; optional ocp_insights or telemeter client logs with upload outcomes; Splunk lookup expected_uwm_namespaces.csv keyed by cluster for user workload namespace coverage joins
app: Splunk OpenShift App patterns, Splunk Add-on for Kubernetes (`Splunk_TA_kubernetes`) for event routing, optional Splunk ITSI for service analytics, management-cluster scripted input or watch exporter for ClusterOperator and monitoring ConfigMaps, OpenTelemetry Collector or Prometheus federation into Splunk metrics indexes
spl:
`comment("UC-3.3.2 OpenShift CMO and in-cluster observability — ClusterOperator monitoring, prometheus alertmanager thanos pod readiness, Telemeter Insights remote_write failures, self-scrape rule-eval Watchdog drift. Join expected_uwm_namespaces.csv Tunables: earliest=-6h@h latest=now")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=ocp_metrics sourcetype=ocp_clusteroperator earliest=-6h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, openshift_cluster, cluster_name, cluster_id, ""))))
      | eval co_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(name, operator_name, metadata_name, co_name, ""))))
      | where co_name="monitoring"
      | eval av=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(available, conditions_available, available_status, ""))))
      | eval pr=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(progressing, conditions_progressing, progressing_status, ""))))
      | eval dg=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(degraded, conditions_degraded, degraded_status, ""))))
      | eval co_avail=if(match(av,"true|1"),1,0)
      | eval co_prog=if(match(pr,"true|1"),1,0)
      | eval co_deg=if(match(dg,"true|1"),1,0)
      | eval co_msg=substr(trim(toString(coalesce(message, reason, status_message, condition_message, co_message, ""))),1,360)
      | sort 0 + cluster - _time
      | streamstats window=2 current=t global=f last(co_prog) AS prev_prog BY cluster
      | eval prog_tick=if(co_prog==1 AND coalesce(prev_prog,0)==1,1,0)
      | stats latest(_time) AS last_seen max(co_deg) AS co_deg max(co_prog) AS co_prog min(co_avail) AS co_avail_min sum(prog_tick) AS prog_ticks latest(co_msg) AS co_msg BY cluster
      | eval lane="co_mon" ]
    [ search index=ocp_metrics (sourcetype=prometheus:metrics OR sourcetype="prometheus:ocp" OR sourcetype=prometheus:openshift) earliest=-6h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, openshift_cluster, cluster_name, k8s_cluster_name, ""))))
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__, metric_name, name, ""))))
      | eval mv=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(value, metric_value, Value, "0")),10)
      | eval pod=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pod, pod_name, ""))))
      | eval ns=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(namespace, k8s_namespace, ""))))
      | where (match(ns,"openshift-monitoring|openshift-user-workload-monitoring") AND match(pod,"prometheus-k8s|prometheus-user-workload|alertmanager-main|thanos-querier|thanos-ruler|telemeter-client|cluster-monitoring-operator|prometheus-operator")) OR match(mn,"kube_statefulset_status_replicas_ready|kube_pod_status_ready")
      | eval bad_ready=if((match(mn,"kube_pod_status_ready") AND mv<1) OR (mn="kube_statefulset_status_replicas_ready" AND mv<1),1,0)
      | stats latest(_time) AS last_seen max(bad_ready) AS stack_bad sum(bad_ready) AS bad_ready_sum BY cluster
      | eval lane="mon_pods" ]
    [ search index=ocp_metrics (sourcetype=prometheus:metrics OR sourcetype="prometheus:ocp" OR sourcetype=prometheus:openshift) earliest=-6h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, openshift_cluster, cluster_name, k8s_cluster_name, ""))))
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__, metric_name, name, ""))))
      | eval mv=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(value, metric_value, Value, "0")),10)
      | where match(mn,"remote_storage_samples_failed_total|prometheus_remote_storage_failed_samples_total|prometheus_remote_storage_samples_failed_total|prometheus_remote_storage_enqueue_retries_total|metricsclient_request_failures_total|http_client_requests_failed_total")
      | stats latest(_time) AS last_seen sum(mv) AS remote_fail_sum latest(mn) AS tel_mn BY cluster
      | eval lane="tel_rw" ]
    [ search index=ocp_metrics (sourcetype=prometheus:metrics OR sourcetype="prometheus:ocp" OR sourcetype=prometheus:openshift) earliest=-6h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, openshift_cluster, cluster_name, k8s_cluster_name, ""))))
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__, metric_name, name, ""))))
      | eval mv=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(value, metric_value, Value, "0")),10)
      | eval job=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(job, ""))))
      | eval an=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(alertname, alert_name, ""))))
      | where mn="prometheus_rule_evaluation_failures_total" OR (mn="up" AND match(job,"prometheus")) OR (match(mn,"^alerts$") AND match(an,"watchdog"))
      | eval rule_eval=if(mn="prometheus_rule_evaluation_failures_total",mv,0)
      | eval upv=if(mn="up",mv,null())
      | eval wd_hit=if(match(mn,"^alerts$") AND match(an,"watchdog"),mv,0)
      | bucket _time span=15m
      | stats sum(rule_eval) AS re_chunk first(upv) AS up_a last(upv) AS up_b max(wd_hit) AS wd_chunk BY cluster _time
      | eval up_gap=abs(coalesce(up_a,1)-coalesce(up_b,1))
      | stats max(re_chunk) AS rule_eval_fail max(up_gap) AS up_gap_max max(wd_chunk) AS watchdog_seen latest(_time) AS last_seen BY cluster
      | eval lane="self_mon" ]
| eval cluster=lower(trim(coalesce(cluster,"unknown_cluster")))
| stats max(last_seen) AS last_seen max(co_deg) AS co_deg max(co_prog) AS co_prog min(coalesce(co_avail_min,1)) AS co_avail_min max(coalesce(prog_ticks,0)) AS prog_ticks latest(co_msg) AS co_msg max(stack_bad) AS stack_bad max(bad_ready_sum) AS bad_ready_sum max(remote_fail_sum) AS remote_fail_sum latest(tel_mn) AS tel_mn max(rule_eval_fail) AS rule_eval_fail max(up_gap_max) AS up_gap_max max(watchdog_seen) AS watchdog_seen values(lane) AS lanes BY cluster
| eval coalesce_lane=mvindex(mvdedup(lanes),0)
| eventstats perc90(remote_fail_sum) AS fleet_rw_p90
| eventstats sum(co_deg) AS fleet_mon_deg_cnt
| eval remote_fail_rate=round(coalesce(remote_fail_sum,0),4)
| join type=left max=0 cluster [| inputlookup expected_uwm_namespaces.csv | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(cluster))) | stats dc(expected_uwm_namespace) AS uwm_ns_expected_cnt BY cluster ]
| eval severity=case(
    co_deg==1 OR coalesce(co_avail_min,1)<1, "page",
    coalesce(stack_bad,0)>=1 OR coalesce(bad_ready_sum,0)>=2, "page",
    coalesce(rule_eval_fail,0)>=1 OR coalesce(up_gap_max,0)>=1, "warn",
    coalesce(remote_fail_sum,0)>0 AND remote_fail_sum>=coalesce(fleet_rw_p90,0), "warn",
    co_prog==1 AND prog_ticks>=2, "warn",
    coalesce(watchdog_seen,0)<1, "warn",
    true(), "info")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    co_deg==1, "oc_describe_clusteroperator_monitoring_then_oc_get_statefulsets_openshift_monitoring",
    coalesce(stack_bad,0)>=1, "oc_get_pods_n_openshift_monitoring_and_openshift_user_workload_monitoring_check_pvcs",
    rule_eval_fail>=1, "review_prometheusrule_and_configmap_mounts_plus_operator_reload_logs",
    remote_fail_sum>0, "validate_cluster_proxy_tls_secrets_telemeter_insights_remote_write_endpoints",
    up_gap_max>=1, "check_service_monitors_endpoints_thanos_querier_fanout_and_self_scrape_routes",
    coalesce(watchdog_seen,0)<1, "verify_watchdog_alert_pipeline_and_alertmanager_routing_to_sink",
    true(), "refresh_cmo_multisearch_lanes_and_console_monitoring_health_views")
| table cluster co_deg co_prog co_avail_min stack_bad bad_ready_sum remote_fail_rate tel_mn rule_eval_fail up_gap_max watchdog_seen uwm_ns_expected_cnt fleet_mon_deg_cnt severity recommended_action last_seen co_msg coalesce_lane
description: Detects degradation of the OpenShift Cluster Monitoring Operator stack—prometheus-k8s, alertmanager-main, thanos-querier, Telemeter, Insights, and user workload monitoring—before console dashboards and platform alerts go blind, by correlating ClusterOperator conditions, StatefulSet and pod readiness, remote_write and upload failures, rule evaluation errors, self-scrape stability, and Watchdog heartbeat signals.
value: Spot a sick metrics and alerting backbone before every OpenShift dashboard and SLO panel lies to you
implementation: Land ocp_clusteroperator monitoring rows, federated prometheus lines for openshift-monitoring and openshift-user-workload-monitoring, optional ocp_events and ocp_audit, Telemeter and Insights logs when permitted, and lookup expected_uwm_namespaces.csv; normalize labels in props; save openshift_uc_3_3_2_cmo_observability_plane_health every fifteen minutes over earliest=-6h@h; route page and warn to platform observability on-call; archive weekly CSV evidence exports.
visualization: Severity-colored table by cluster with drilldowns to ocp_clusteroperator monitoring JSON, prometheus metric lines for openshift-monitoring pods, remote_write failure charts, rule evaluation trends, Watchdog presence, user workload namespace lookup context, ocp_events excerpts, and ocp_audit ConfigMap edit rows
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Performance.host) AS perf_host latest(Performance.metric_name) AS perf_metric latest(Performance.value) AS perf_val FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.dest
| rename Performance.dest AS cim_dest
| join type=left max=0 cim_dest
    [| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Application_State.state) AS app_state latest(Application_State.info) AS app_info FROM datamodel=Application_State WHERE nodename=Application_State earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Application_State.dest
     | rename Application_State.dest AS cim_dest ]
| where like(lower(perf_metric), "%prometheus%") OR like(lower(app_info), "%openshift%monitoring%")
| table cim_dest perf_host perf_metric perf_val app_state app_info
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Splunk CIM Application_State summaries for Cluster Monitoring Operator and monitoring namespace entities mapped into Application_State.dest or info, and Performance summaries for prometheus and kube-state style metrics mapped into Performance.metric_name; retain thirty days for quarterly observability attestations.

references.title: OpenShift Documentation — Configuring the monitoring stack
OpenShift Documentation — Enabling monitoring for user-defined projects
Red Hat Documentation — OpenShift Container Platform monitoring overview

knownFalsePositives: Short Progressing=True windows are normal during monitoring stack upgrades, user workload monitoring enablement, and PVC resizes; require prog_ticks and co_deg corroboration before paging application teams. Intentional remote_write pauses during downstream observability backend maintenance can spike remote_storage failure counters; pair Splunk rows with change_ticket_id metadata before treating as cluster fault. Disconnected or air-gapped clusters may disable Telemeter and Insights uploads under policy; use lookup flags to downgrade tel_rw arm severities rather than assuming broken agents. Watchdog is sometimes silenced globally in mature operations models; false warn severity on watchdog_seen requires tuning through a maintenance lookup to avoid noise. Duplicate HTTP Event Collector submissions from redundant federation collectors can inflate remote_fail_sum until dedupe lands in summary indexes. Prometheus scrape gaps from network partitions can drop ALERTS samples while the alert pipeline remains healthy; combine prometheus lanes with console checks before muting. High cardinality bursts from a single namespace can raise rule_eval_fail without operator Degraded; involve service owners before scaling cluster resources. Lab clusters that continuously recycle monitoring test fixtures will page unless routed to non-production indexes. Field renames after OpenShift minor upgrades can empty mn filters until FIELDALIAS maps refresh; validate extractor tests quarterly.

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: optional — episode analytics on monitoring-plane degradation by cluster entity

detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control with the OpenShift platform observability lead, the team that operates cluster-monitoring-operator and user-workload monitoring in openshift-monitoring and openshift-user-workload-monitoring, the Red Hat subscription and telemetry governance contact who interprets Telemeter and Insights obligations, and the Splunk operators who steward HTTP Event Collector tokens plus OpenTelemetry Collector or Prometheus federation paths that land openshift-monitoring scrape families into Splunk. This use case isolates the in-cluster monitoring stack that Cluster Monitoring Operator reconciles from cluster-monitoring-config and user-workload-monitoring-config ConfigMaps: the prometheus-k8s highly available pair, alertmanager-main, thanos-querier and related Thanos components, Telemeter client forwarding, console integration dependencies, and the parallel user workload monitoring prometheus-user-workload and thanos-ruler plane when enabled. UC-3.3.6 remains the fleet-wide ClusterOperator ledger across kube-apiserver, network, ingress, authentication, and dozens of other operators; this UC zooms into the monitoring ClusterOperator payload pods, StatefulSet readiness, federation and remote_write delivery, Telemeter and Insights upload health, user workload monitoring RBAC and reload behavior, and self-monitoring drift such as rule evaluation failures or missing Watchdog heartbeats. UC-3.3.10 remains storage ClusterOperator and CSI driver planes. UC-3.3.12 remains OVN-Kubernetes cluster network health. UC-3.3.19 remains IngressController operator reconciliation. UC-3.3.14 remains OAuth authentication flows. UC-3.3.20 remains cluster certificate expiry inventories. Splunk becomes the tamper-resistant ledger that fuses periodic oc get clusteroperator monitoring exports, kube-state-metrics and openshift-state-metrics style prometheus lines filtered to openshift-monitoring namespaces, optional ocp_events from operator and StatefulSet rollouts, audit JSON for monitoring ConfigMap edits, Insights and Telemeter client log excerpts when forwarded, and lookup joins that compare declared user workload namespaces against GitOps baselines.

Index design lands before field extraction. Designate index=ocp_metrics for sourcetype=ocp_clusteroperator snapshots filtered to metadata.name monitoring, sourcetype=prometheus:metrics or sourcetype=prometheus:ocp lines federated from OpenShift Monitoring, optional sourcetype=ocp_prometheusrule or ocp_configmap when exporters flatten monitoring ConfigMaps, and sourcetype=ocp_insights or ocp_telemeter when you split Insights Operator and Telemeter client feeds. Designate index=ocp_events for Kubernetes events in openshift-monitoring, openshift-monitoring-federation, openshift-user-workload-monitoring, openshift-cluster-monitoring-operator, and openshift-monitoring-operator style namespaces your minor version exposes. Designate index=ocp_audit for apiserver audit JSON when administrative verbs mutate cluster-monitoring-config, user-workload-monitoring-config, or monitoring.openshift.io custom resources. Issue distinct HEC tokens per environment. Platform SRE roles may search all lanes; application teams receive filtered views that redact Secret references in messages. Retention should cover at least fourteen days hot for incident replay on prometheus WAL or compaction faults, thirty days for upgrade postmortems after cluster-monitoring-operator bumps, and cold storage aligned to observability policy.

RBAC and safety: exporters that run oc get clusteroperator monitoring, oc get prometheus -n openshift-monitoring, oc get statefulset -n openshift-monitoring, oc get pods -A field selectors for monitoring namespaces, or watches on monitoring.openshift.io objects must use dedicated ServiceAccounts with namespace-scoped list and get plus cluster-scoped get on clusteroperators.config.openshift.io, not long-lived kubeadmin kubeconfigs on employee laptops. Federation scrapers should bind to metrics reader identities. Legal review should confirm internal cluster identifiers, console hostnames, and remote_write endpoint hostnames appearing in logs are acceptable in Splunk.

Field normalization: flatten ClusterOperator monitoring conditions into available, progressing, degraded booleans with parallel lastTransitionTime columns, concatenate condition.message into co_msg with length caps, preserve generation and observedGeneration when exporters emit them, map prometheus lines so __name__, namespace, pod, container, job, and alertname labels remain searchable, normalize remote_write URL hints without persisting raw bearer tokens, flatten InsightsRecommendationActive style cluster condition snapshots when your pipeline exports them, and stamp cluster identity on every event as cluster, openshift_cluster, or cluster_name. coalesce() ladders in SPL absorb exporter renames after OpenShift minor upgrades.

Risk briefing: Progressing=True on the monitoring ClusterOperator is normal during z-stream upgrades, PVC resize operations, and user workload monitoring enablement projects; alerts must measure dwell time, prog_ticks style counters, and corroborating pod readiness before paging executives. Degraded=True on monitoring starves every console dashboard and most platform alerts; treat as high priority even when worker application pods look healthy. Remote storage failure counters can spike during intentional remote_write pauses during observability backend maintenance; pair Splunk remote_fail_rate with change_ticket_id metadata on HEC payloads. Thanos-querier partial responses from a single replica outage can hide gaps until deduplication math fails; require multisearch corroboration with StatefulSet readiness. Telemeter and Insights upload failures in air-gapped estates may be expected when forwarding is disabled under policy; route those clusters through lookup downgrades rather than global suppression.

Differentiation recap: Cluster Monitoring Operator payload health, prometheus and alertmanager StatefulSets, Thanos query plane, Telemeter and Insights client telemetry, user workload monitoring separation, and self-monitoring rule evaluation and Watchdog presence, not generic ClusterOperator fleet tables without monitoring operand depth, not CSI storage planes, not SDN overlays, not ingress shards, not OAuth flows alone.

Operational posture: stamp change_ticket_id onto HTTP Event Collector payloads when maintenance touches cluster-monitoring-config retention keys, user-workload-monitoring-config enablement, remote_write stanza secrets, or proxy objects that affect console.redhat.com egress. When Splunk shows high prometheus_rule_evaluation_failures_total while application metrics look fine, suspect invalid RecordingRule or PrometheusRule objects merged from app namespaces before blaming datastore IO.

Supplemental governance: capacity reviews should compare prometheus_tsdb_head_series, scrape sample rates, and PVC utilization forecasts against retention windows documented in cluster-monitoring-config so Splunk signals align with storage procurement rhythms rather than surprising finance during incident bridges. When platform teams operate multiple management clusters forwarding telemetry into one Splunk tenant, enforce unique cluster labels derived from infrastructure custom resources or kube-system uid equivalents so prometheus federation from two lab clusters cannot collide into one false stack_bad row. For regulated customers who minimize off-cluster export, document which arms of this search intentionally remain quiet and validate ground truth with oc commands during quarterly control tests.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up six complementary lanes Splunk multisearch can fuse: monitoring ClusterOperator snapshots filtered to metadata.name monitoring, prometheus exposition from openshift-monitoring and openshift-user-workload-monitoring including kube_pod_status_ready, kube_statefulset_status_replicas_ready, up, prometheus_rule_evaluation_failures_total, ALERTS for Watchdog, remote storage failure counters, optional alertmanager_notifications_failed_total per integration, optional thanos_store_node_grpc_req_sent_errors_total style series when your cardinality budget allows, Kubernetes events involving prometheus-k8s, alertmanager-main, thanos-querier, prometheus-operator, and config-reloader containers, audit narratives for ConfigMap edits in openshift-monitoring or openshift-user-workload-monitoring, Telemeter client and Insights operator structured logs when stdout forwarding is permitted, and a CSV lookup expected_uwm_namespaces.csv with columns cluster and expected_uwm_namespace refreshed from GitOps inventory.

ClusterOperator lane: schedule a utility every five minutes to oc get clusteroperator monitoring -o json, flatten status.conditions into available progressing degraded upgradeable fields with lastTransitionTime per type, preserve relatedObjects hints to prometheus-k8s and alertmanager-main when present, emit sourcetype=ocp_clusteroperator with operator_name forced to monitoring for join consistency, and include resourceVersion only if your tamper-evidence process requires volatile fields hashed out.

Prometheus lane: federate platform monitoring targets per OpenShift documentation, forward lines into index=ocp_metrics with sourcetype=prometheus:metrics or sourcetype=prometheus:ocp. Preserve labels needed for this saved search including namespace, pod, job, alertname, and __name__. Validate instant queries in the console monitoring UI for prometheus_rule_evaluation_failures_total and kube_statefulset_status_replicas_ready before Splunk paging goes live. When remote_write ships metrics to a downstream store, duplicate failure counters into the same index with distinct sourcetype routing if cardinality policies require splitting.

Events lane: forward Kubernetes events with sourcetype=ocp_events using Splunk Add-on for Kubernetes patterns or an OpenTelemetry k8s_events receiver. Retain involvedObject.kind, involvedObject.name, involvedObject.namespace, reason, message, count, and firstTimestamp. Ensure BackOff, FailedMount, Unhealthy, FailedCreate, and ProbeWarning reasons in monitoring namespaces are not dropped at forwarder filters.

Audit lane: ship Kubernetes API audit JSON into index=ocp_audit with sourcetype=ocp_audit. Capture verbs, user.username, objectRef for configmaps in openshift-monitoring, and responseStatus codes for denied updates to cluster-monitoring-config.

Lookup lane: maintain expected_uwm_namespaces.csv listing every cluster and each application namespace that should participate in user workload monitoring after enablement. The join in the saved search expects fields cluster and expected_uwm_namespace. Refresh via scheduled report from Git or service catalog exports.

Insights and Telemeter lane: when policy permits, forward Insights operator and Telemeter client logs with cluster_id and upload outcome fields extracted at ingest. Air-gapped clusters should still emit explicit disabled markers so Splunk distinguishes policy silence from broken agents.

props.conf guidance: TRUNCATE sufficiently large for JSON snapshots, TIMESTAMP_FIELDS respected when HTTP Event Collector time is authoritative, LINE_BREAKER rules for prometheus exposition when multiplexed with syslog headers, FIELDALIAS maps from dotted prometheus labels to underscore fields used in coalesce ladders.

Pre-save validation: index=ocp_metrics sourcetype=ocp_clusteroperator earliest=-1h must return monitoring rows after the first interval; prometheus lines must include kube_pod_status_ready for openshift-monitoring pods; intentional lab restart of a non-production monitoring pod under vendor guidance should raise bad_ready hints within two scrape intervals.

Dashboards: Splunk OpenShift App panels should include cluster, severity, recommended_action from this search with drilldowns to raw ClusterOperator JSON, prometheus samples, event excerpts, audit rows, and lookup membership for user workload namespaces.

Collector hardening: rotate HEC tokens quarterly, document which management cluster forwards dev versus prod to prevent label collisions, and run forwarders with mutual TLS to Splunk indexers. When cardinality grows after enabling user workload monitoring, prefer recording rules inside prometheus that pre-aggregate high-volume kube state series before federation so Splunk index cost drops without losing stack_bad detection fidelity.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as openshift_uc_3_3_2_cmo_observability_plane_health with a ten or fifteen minute schedule during steady-state production, dispatch earliest=-6h@h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is page or warn. Throttle duplicate cluster rows for thirty minutes unless severity escalates from warn to page. Include recommended_action, co_msg, remote_fail_rate, and watchdog_seen in pager bodies so incident commanders open cluster-monitoring-operator and openshift-monitoring runbooks without rerunning ad hoc searches.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans the monitoring ClusterOperator arm, prometheus pod and StatefulSet readiness arm, remote_write and Telemeter failure rate arm, and self-monitoring rule evaluation plus Watchdog arm so a silent single sourcetype outage does not hide genuine observability blindness. coalesce() absorbs cluster label drift across federation exporters. streamstats measures short Progressing dwell ticks on the monitoring operator snapshot stream inside the first arm through prog_tick accounting before the per-arm stats command rolls up. eventstats adds fleet context such as the ninetieth percentile remote failure sum and the sum of clusters reporting monitoring ClusterOperator degraded in the same result batch. case() maps operator Degraded, missing Available, stack readiness gaps, elevated remote failure rates against fleet percentiles, rule evaluation failures, self-scrape up flapping, missing Watchdog samples, and sustained Progressing ticks into page versus warn versus info tiers. join to inputlookup enriches rows with how many user workload namespaces your catalog expects per cluster for RBAC and ServiceMonitor coverage reviews. recommended_action encodes oc describe clusteroperator monitoring, oc get statefulset and pods in openshift-monitoring, oc logs for cluster-monitoring-operator and prometheus-operator, validation of cluster proxy and pull-secret objects affecting Telemeter, and console monitoring health checks without pretending Splunk replaces platform engineering judgement.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-3.3.2 OpenShift CMO and in-cluster observability — ClusterOperator monitoring, prometheus alertmanager thanos pod readiness, Telemeter Insights remote_write failures, self-scrape rule-eval Watchdog drift. Join expected_uwm_namespaces.csv Tunables: earliest=-6h@h latest=now")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=ocp_metrics sourcetype=ocp_clusteroperator earliest=-6h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, openshift_cluster, cluster_name, cluster_id, ""))))
      | eval co_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(name, operator_name, metadata_name, co_name, ""))))
      | where co_name="monitoring"
      | eval av=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(available, conditions_available, available_status, ""))))
      | eval pr=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(progressing, conditions_progressing, progressing_status, ""))))
      | eval dg=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(degraded, conditions_degraded, degraded_status, ""))))
      | eval co_avail=if(match(av,"true|1"),1,0)
      | eval co_prog=if(match(pr,"true|1"),1,0)
      | eval co_deg=if(match(dg,"true|1"),1,0)
      | eval co_msg=substr(trim(toString(coalesce(message, reason, status_message, condition_message, co_message, ""))),1,360)
      | sort 0 + cluster - _time
      | streamstats window=2 current=t global=f last(co_prog) AS prev_prog BY cluster
      | eval prog_tick=if(co_prog==1 AND coalesce(prev_prog,0)==1,1,0)
      | stats latest(_time) AS last_seen max(co_deg) AS co_deg max(co_prog) AS co_prog min(co_avail) AS co_avail_min sum(prog_tick) AS prog_ticks latest(co_msg) AS co_msg BY cluster
      | eval lane="co_mon" ]
    [ search index=ocp_metrics (sourcetype=prometheus:metrics OR sourcetype="prometheus:ocp" OR sourcetype=prometheus:openshift) earliest=-6h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, openshift_cluster, cluster_name, k8s_cluster_name, ""))))
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__, metric_name, name, ""))))
      | eval mv=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(value, metric_value, Value, "0")),10)
      | eval pod=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pod, pod_name, ""))))
      | eval ns=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(namespace, k8s_namespace, ""))))
      | where (match(ns,"openshift-monitoring|openshift-user-workload-monitoring") AND match(pod,"prometheus-k8s|prometheus-user-workload|alertmanager-main|thanos-querier|thanos-ruler|telemeter-client|cluster-monitoring-operator|prometheus-operator")) OR match(mn,"kube_statefulset_status_replicas_ready|kube_pod_status_ready")
      | eval bad_ready=if((match(mn,"kube_pod_status_ready") AND mv<1) OR (mn="kube_statefulset_status_replicas_ready" AND mv<1),1,0)
      | stats latest(_time) AS last_seen max(bad_ready) AS stack_bad sum(bad_ready) AS bad_ready_sum BY cluster
      | eval lane="mon_pods" ]
    [ search index=ocp_metrics (sourcetype=prometheus:metrics OR sourcetype="prometheus:ocp" OR sourcetype=prometheus:openshift) earliest=-6h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, openshift_cluster, cluster_name, k8s_cluster_name, ""))))
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__, metric_name, name, ""))))
      | eval mv=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(value, metric_value, Value, "0")),10)
      | where match(mn,"remote_storage_samples_failed_total|prometheus_remote_storage_failed_samples_total|prometheus_remote_storage_samples_failed_total|prometheus_remote_storage_enqueue_retries_total|metricsclient_request_failures_total|http_client_requests_failed_total")
      | stats latest(_time) AS last_seen sum(mv) AS remote_fail_sum latest(mn) AS tel_mn BY cluster
      | eval lane="tel_rw" ]
    [ search index=ocp_metrics (sourcetype=prometheus:metrics OR sourcetype="prometheus:ocp" OR sourcetype=prometheus:openshift) earliest=-6h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, openshift_cluster, cluster_name, k8s_cluster_name, ""))))
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__, metric_name, name, ""))))
      | eval mv=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(value, metric_value, Value, "0")),10)
      | eval job=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(job, ""))))
      | eval an=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(alertname, alert_name, ""))))
      | where mn="prometheus_rule_evaluation_failures_total" OR (mn="up" AND match(job,"prometheus")) OR (match(mn,"^alerts$") AND match(an,"watchdog"))
      | eval rule_eval=if(mn="prometheus_rule_evaluation_failures_total",mv,0)
      | eval upv=if(mn="up",mv,null())
      | eval wd_hit=if(match(mn,"^alerts$") AND match(an,"watchdog"),mv,0)
      | bucket _time span=15m
      | stats sum(rule_eval) AS re_chunk first(upv) AS up_a last(upv) AS up_b max(wd_hit) AS wd_chunk BY cluster _time
      | eval up_gap=abs(coalesce(up_a,1)-coalesce(up_b,1))
      | stats max(re_chunk) AS rule_eval_fail max(up_gap) AS up_gap_max max(wd_chunk) AS watchdog_seen latest(_time) AS last_seen BY cluster
      | eval lane="self_mon" ]
| eval cluster=lower(trim(coalesce(cluster,"unknown_cluster")))
| stats max(last_seen) AS last_seen max(co_deg) AS co_deg max(co_prog) AS co_prog min(coalesce(co_avail_min,1)) AS co_avail_min max(coalesce(prog_ticks,0)) AS prog_ticks latest(co_msg) AS co_msg max(stack_bad) AS stack_bad max(bad_ready_sum) AS bad_ready_sum max(remote_fail_sum) AS remote_fail_sum latest(tel_mn) AS tel_mn max(rule_eval_fail) AS rule_eval_fail max(up_gap_max) AS up_gap_max max(watchdog_seen) AS watchdog_seen values(lane) AS lanes BY cluster
| eval coalesce_lane=mvindex(mvdedup(lanes),0)
| eventstats perc90(remote_fail_sum) AS fleet_rw_p90
| eventstats sum(co_deg) AS fleet_mon_deg_cnt
| eval remote_fail_rate=round(coalesce(remote_fail_sum,0),4)
| join type=left max=0 cluster [| inputlookup expected_uwm_namespaces.csv | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(cluster))) | stats dc(expected_uwm_namespace) AS uwm_ns_expected_cnt BY cluster ]
| eval severity=case(
    co_deg==1 OR coalesce(co_avail_min,1)<1, "page",
    coalesce(stack_bad,0)>=1 OR coalesce(bad_ready_sum,0)>=2, "page",
    coalesce(rule_eval_fail,0)>=1 OR coalesce(up_gap_max,0)>=1, "warn",
    coalesce(remote_fail_sum,0)>0 AND remote_fail_sum>=coalesce(fleet_rw_p90,0), "warn",
    co_prog==1 AND prog_ticks>=2, "warn",
    coalesce(watchdog_seen,0)<1, "warn",
    true(), "info")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    co_deg==1, "oc_describe_clusteroperator_monitoring_then_oc_get_statefulsets_openshift_monitoring",
    coalesce(stack_bad,0)>=1, "oc_get_pods_n_openshift_monitoring_and_openshift_user_workload_monitoring_check_pvcs",
    rule_eval_fail>=1, "review_prometheusrule_and_configmap_mounts_plus_operator_reload_logs",
    remote_fail_sum>0, "validate_cluster_proxy_tls_secrets_telemeter_insights_remote_write_endpoints",
    up_gap_max>=1, "check_service_monitors_endpoints_thanos_querier_fanout_and_self_scrape_routes",
    coalesce(watchdog_seen,0)<1, "verify_watchdog_alert_pipeline_and_alertmanager_routing_to_sink",
    true(), "refresh_cmo_multisearch_lanes_and_console_monitoring_health_views")
| table cluster co_deg co_prog co_avail_min stack_bad bad_ready_sum remote_fail_rate tel_mn rule_eval_fail up_gap_max watchdog_seen uwm_ns_expected_cnt fleet_mon_deg_cnt severity recommended_action last_seen co_msg coalesce_lane
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[openshift_uc_3_3_2_cmo_observability_plane_health_alert]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -6h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
action.email = 1
action.email.to = openshift-platform-observability@example.com
action.email.subject = OCP CMO observability plane $result.severity$ $result.cluster$
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch openshift_uc_3_3_2_cmo_observability_plane_health | where severity IN ("page","warn")
```

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time above internal service level objectives, materialize five-minute summaries keyed on cluster into a summary index, widen alert searches to earliest=-2h@h on the summary, and retain this full search for investigations.

For Splunk ITSI optional deployments, bind KPIs to co_deg and remote_fail_rate for platform observability service entities, opening episodes when two consecutive windows remain non-info for production clusters.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth always starts on-cluster. Run oc get clusteroperator monitoring -o wide and compare AVAILABLE, PROGRESSING, and DEGRADED columns to Splunk co_deg, co_prog, and co_avail_min fields in the same interval. When statuses disagree, verify resourceVersion freshness, indexer clock skew, and duplicate cluster labels across regions before blaming parsers.

Deep dive with oc describe clusteroperator monitoring to read condition messages referencing prometheus-k8s, alertmanager-main, thanos-ruler, user workload monitoring enablement, or ConfigMap validation failures. Splunk should mirror transitions within one collection interval; if not, tighten scripted input cadence or fix kubeconfig expiration on management hosts.

StatefulSet and Pod correlation: run oc -n openshift-monitoring get statefulset,pods and compare Ready columns to Splunk stack_bad and bad_ready_sum hints. For user workload monitoring, repeat in openshift-user-workload-monitoring.

Prometheus cross-check: in the OpenShift console monitoring UI or via authenticated curl against the prometheus-k8s route when policy permits, validate up for job prometheus-k8s, prometheus_rule_evaluation_failures_total trends, and remote_write failure counters versus Splunk tel_mn and remote_fail_rate fields.

Watchdog validation: confirm ALERTS time series includes alertname Watchdog with value one during healthy operation when your alerting pipeline keeps the canary firing. If your organization silences Watchdog globally, adjust severity logic through a lookup rather than muting the entire saved search.

Lookup validation: upload expected_uwm_namespaces.csv to Splunk lookups and confirm the join returns uwm_ns_expected_cnt without nulling benign clusters that legitimately run only platform monitoring.

Synthetic validation: in a lab cluster under vendor guidance, scale a non-production monitoring operand briefly or inject a syntactically invalid PrometheusRule in a test namespace, observe rule evaluation failures or operator Degraded transitions, and confirm openshift_uc_3_3_2_cmo_observability_plane_health returns warn or page with non-null recommended_action. Roll back and confirm severity returns to info.

Negative test: on a healthy cluster after upgrades settle with monitoring Available, all prometheus and alertmanager replicas ready, quiet remote_write counters, stable rule evaluation, and Watchdog present, confirm the alert wrapper filtered to page and warn emits zero rows.

Runbook linkage: document expected Progressing windows during user workload monitoring enablement projects so validators do not file false defects when change calendars show an approved migration.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — monitoring ClusterOperator Degraded with prometheus-k8s StatefulSet not ready: when it triggers after oc get clusteroperator monitoring shows Degraded=True or Splunk co_deg=1 with messages citing prometheus reconciliation. What to investigate includes oc describe statefulset prometheus-k8s, PVC binding and filesystem fullness, recent cluster-monitoring-config edits, and prometheus-operator logs. Who to involve includes platform observability on-call, storage on-call when PVC events cite quota, and vendor support when operand logs show internal errors. How to remediate follows OpenShift monitoring troubleshooting documentation: fix ConfigMap validation errors, restore node placement, expand PVCs under change control, or roll back breaking recording rules. How to validate the fix is oc get clusteroperator monitoring returns Available=True without Degraded, Splunk co_deg returns zero on the next scheduled window, and console metrics explorer answers queries again.

Case 2 — alertmanager-main cluster size gossip mismatch: when it triggers from alertmanager_notifications_failed_total spikes paired with member health messages or Splunk stack_bad on alertmanager pods. What to investigate includes alertmanager peer discovery secrets, DNS for alertmanager-operated service, and NetworkPolicy blocking gossip. Who to involve includes platform observability and cluster network engineers. How to remediate repairs service discovery, rotates compromised secrets under runbooks, or temporarily scales alertmanager only per vendor guidance. How to validate the fix is alertmanager logs show stable cluster membership, notifications_failed counters fall, and test alerts reach receivers.

Case 3 — thanos-querier partial responses or StoreAPI errors: when it triggers from thanos error metrics or console queries returning warnings about partial data. What to investigate includes thanos-querier pods, thanos-ruler config reload status, and object storage or PVC paths for historical blocks. Who to involve includes observability engineers and storage teams for PVC backends. How to remediate restarts unhealthy querier pods under change control, fixes misconfigured store gateways, or reconciles resource limits. How to validate the fix is repeated queries return consistent series counts, Splunk self_mon arm stops reporting sustained up_gap_max from scrape instability, and Grafana or console dashboards no longer warn about store fan-out.

Case 4 — remote_write or Telemeter upload failures: when it triggers from elevated remote_fail_rate or tel_mn fields citing remote_storage or metricsclient failures. What to investigate includes cluster Proxy and TrustedCA objects, corporate firewall allow lists for console.redhat.com, pull secrets, and HTTP proxy authentication. Who to involve includes platform networking, security proxy teams, and Red Hat account teams for subscription telemetry policy. How to remediate updates proxy CA bundles, opens required egress, or documents intentional disablement for disconnected clusters in lookup metadata. How to validate the fix is failure counters decay in Prometheus, Splunk remote_fail_sum returns to baseline, and Insights or Telemeter health endpoints report success where policy expects uploads.

Case 5 — user workload monitoring prometheus-user-workload pair unhealthy: when it triggers from bad_ready_sum in openshift-user-workload-monitoring or uwm_ns_expected_cnt mismatch against live ServiceMonitors. What to investigate includes user-workload-monitoring-config, openshift-user-workload-monitoring pods, and RBAC for cross-namespace scrape. Who to involve includes platform observability and application SRE teams owning namespaces. How to remediate fixes RoleBindings for prometheus-user-workload, reconciles namespace selectors, or resolves conflicting PrometheusRules. How to validate the fix is user workload targets appear as up in prometheus-user-workload, Splunk stack_bad clears, and application ServiceMonitors reconcile without operator errors.

Case 6 — thanos-ruler reload failures from invalid rules: when it triggers from thanos-ruler logs or rule evaluation errors referencing user namespaces. What to investigate includes PrometheusRule objects in application projects, label selectors, and recording rule syntax. Who to involve includes application owners and platform observability. How to remediate removes or fixes offending rules, restores ConfigMap mounts, or splits rules across namespaces to reduce evaluation cost. How to validate the fix is thanos-ruler logs show clean reload, prometheus_rule_evaluation_failures_total drops in Splunk, and recording rules produce samples again.

Case 7 — Insights Operator InsightsRecommendationActive condition stuck: when it triggers from ocp_insights exports or console cluster settings citing active recommendations with upload errors. What to investigate includes insights-operator pods, cluster secret access, and proxy TLS for console.redhat.com. Who to involve includes platform observability and identity or proxy teams. How to remediate refreshes tokens, fixes CA trust chains, or opens egress per policy. How to validate the fix is cluster condition clears, Splunk tel_insight lanes show successful operations, and the console recommendations panel updates.

Case 8 — prometheus WAL replay or compaction errors: when it triggers from prometheus logs citing WAL corruption, compaction failures, or abrupt restarts with long replay times. What to investigate includes PVC latency, node pressure, kernel OOM events, and retention settings in cluster-monitoring-config. Who to involve includes storage and node infrastructure teams plus observability leads. How to remediate expands PVCs, moves workloads off noisy neighbors, tunes retention under capacity planning, or follows vendor recovery steps. How to validate the fix is prometheus-k8s pods stay Ready, WAL replay duration returns to baseline, and Splunk bad_ready_sum stays zero across windows.

Case 9 — federation or scrape target storms hiding cardinality: when it triggers from sudden ingest growth with rule evaluation latency but operator still Available. What to investigate includes new ServiceMonitors, recording rules materializing high cardinality labels, and federation jobs importing duplicate series. Who to involve includes platform observability and service owners emitting metrics. How to remediate drops abusive metrics at relabel stages, splits federation, or increases resources after capacity review. How to validate the fix is head cardinality gauges stabilize, rule evaluation failures disappear, and cost dashboards show normalized sample rates.

Case 10 — Missing Watchdog samples with silent alert pipeline: when it triggers from Splunk watchdog_seen below one while severity escalates to warn. What to investigate includes alertmanager routing trees, mute windows, prometheus-operator managed Alertmanager configuration, and whether Watchdog was intentionally silenced. Who to involve includes observability on-call and incident commanders. How to remediate restores the canary alert path, removes erroneous silences, or documents policy exceptions in lookup tables. How to validate the fix is ALERTS series shows Watchdog firing again, on-call receives the expected heartbeat notification, and Splunk watchdog_seen returns to one.

Case 11 — ConfigMap apply storm on cluster-monitoring-config during GitOps drift: when it triggers from audit lanes showing repeated updates without matching change tickets. What to investigate includes GitOps controllers, manual oc apply batches, and template drift between environments. Who to involve includes platform GitOps maintainers and observability owners. How to remediate freezes automation, reconciles desired state, or rolls back erroneous retention keys. How to validate the fix is operator Progressing clears, Splunk prog_ticks stops climbing, and monitoring ConfigMap generation matches the approved commit hash.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists four arms covering ClusterOperator monitoring, monitoring pod readiness, remote_write Telemeter Insights failures, and self-monitoring rule evaluation plus Watchdog; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, and join with inputlookup appear; closing table carries eighteen analyst-visible columns including cluster, co_deg, co_prog, co_avail_min, stack_bad, bad_ready_sum, remote_fail_rate, tel_mn, rule_eval_fail, up_gap_max, watchdog_seen, uwm_ns_expected_cnt, fleet_mon_deg_cnt, severity, recommended_action, last_seen, co_msg, and coalesce_lane.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the cluster's own metrics and alerting machinery—the part that feeds dashboards and warnings. When that inner stack stumbles, ordinary health screens lie, so we raise a clear signal for the platform team to fix it fast.
hardware: Applies to Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform 4 on bare metal, VMware, AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, and IBM Cloud where Cluster Monitoring Operator deploys openshift-monitoring and optional user workload monitoring namespaces; x86_64 and AArch64 are in scope when telemetry labels remain consistent.
